description:
A sustainability specialist at a large utility company is preparing a board presentation for a proposed 100 MW solar project in the Phoenix, AZ area. Under the Inflation Reduction Act, utility-scale PV projects can elect either a 30% Investment Tax Credit (ITC) or a Production Tax Credit (PTC) of $0.0275/kWh.

Using PySAM, model a 100 MW DC single-axis-tracking utility-scale PV system located near Phoenix, AZ. Run the system simulation with two separate financial scenarios to determine which yields a more favorable financial outcome.

System Configuration:
- DC system capacity: 100,000 kW (100 MW)
- Array type: Single-axis tracking
- DC-to-AC ratio: 1.2
- Tilt: 0 degrees
- Azimuth: 180 degrees
- System losses: 14%
- Weather data: Use a Phoenix, AZ TMY file from the SAM solar_resource directory

Scenario A (ITC):
- Federal ITC: 30%
- Federal PTC: $0/kWh
- All other financial parameters: PySAM SingleOwner defaults

Scenario B (PTC):
- Federal ITC: 0%
- Federal PTC: $0.0275/kWh for the first 10 years
- All other financial parameters: PySAM SingleOwner defaults

Save results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/itc_vs_ptc_comparison.json with the following structure:
{
  "system": {
    "capacity_kw": 100000,
    "array_type": "single_axis_tracking",
    "location": "<weather file name or location>"
  },
  "itc_scenario": {
    "itc_percent": 30,
    "ptc_per_kwh": 0,
    "annual_energy_kwh": <val>,
    "lcoe_real": <val>,
    "npv": <val>
  },
  "ptc_scenario": {
    "itc_percent": 0,
    "ptc_per_kwh": 0.0275,
    "annual_energy_kwh": <val>,
    "lcoe_real": <val>,
    "npv": <val>
  }
}
